steps.title: Expand the factored form and match coefficients
steps.content:
Expand $(hx + k)(x + j)$:
$$hx^2 + (hj + k)x + kj$$
This must equal $4x^2 + bx - 45$. Comparing coefficients:
  • Coefficient of $x^2$: $h = 4$
  • Coefficient of $x$: $hj + k = b$
  • Constant term: $kj = -45$

steps.title: Use kj = −45 to identify which expression is always an integer
steps.content:
Since $k$ and $j$ are integers with $kj = -45$, $k$ must be a divisor of $45$.

Check each choice:
  • Choice C: $\frac{45}{h} = \frac{45}{4}$ — not an integer since $4 \nmid 45$.
  • Choice A: $\frac{b}{h} = \frac{4j+k}{4}$ — depends on $k$. If $k = 5$ and $j = -9$, then $b = 4(-9) + 5 = -31$, so $\frac{-31}{4}$ is not an integer.
  • Choice B: $\frac{b}{k} = \frac{4j+k}{k} = 4\frac{j}{k} + 1$ — not always an integer. If $k = 5$ and $j = -9$, then $4\frac{-9}{5} + 1 = \frac{-36+5}{5} = \frac{-31}{5}$, not an integer.
  • Choice D: $\frac{45}{k}$ — since $k$ divides $45$, this is always an integer. For any divisor of $45$ (e.g., $\pm 1, \pm 3, \pm 5, \pm 9, \pm 15, \pm 45$), the quotient $\frac{45}{k}$ is an integer.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
